verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II membrane protein resident in the Golgi apparatus. Its N-terminal hydrophobic signal sequence directs it to the Golgi, where it remains as a Golgi-membrane-resident glycosyltransferase. Type II membrane proteins in the secretory pathway (signal sequence uncleaved, catalytic domain facing the lumen) are endomembrane-resident, not surface-accessible. The protein has no documented trafficking to the plasma membrane, no ectodomain shedding, and no cell-surface therapeutic engagement. It is not accessible from the extracellular face.
